summary:
Varicella-zoster virus (VZV) infection during early pregnancy causes congenital varicella syndrome, characterized by limbohypoplasia and distinct skin scarring. It is uniquely among the options linked to both findings based on clinical literature.  

\boxed{A} The correct answer is $\boxed{\text{A}}$.  

The summary should be exactly two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant associations:

Varicella-zoster virus is strongly associated with congenital varicella syndrome featuring limb reduction defects and cutaneous scarring. Among all tested intrapartum infections, this condition most consistently demonstrates both pathologies described in the query.\boxed{A}

Okay, I see multiple versions were generated — let me now produce one clean, concise version meeting ALL requirements strictly:

---

Varicella-zoster virus infection is known to cause congenital varicella syndrome, which includes characteristic limb hypoplasia and skin scarring. These specific manifestations make it the only intrauterine infection reliably tied to both conditions identified in the question.  

$\boxed{A}$
